description: China has unveiled stringent new regulations to bolster oversight of its overseas investments, particularly in critical tech sectors like AI and semiconductors. These rules, effective Wednesday, aim to safeguard national security and technological capabilities amidst escalating US competition. While designed to enhance outward investment quality, they could complicate global expansion for Chinese firms and access to Chinese capital for foreign partners, raising concerns about technology transfers and broader geopolitical implications.

Swann described the young batter as "almost unplayable" and noted that few bowlers have found success against him.  While England's bowlers will be tested, Jofra Archer might possess an edge due to his IPL experience with Sooryavanshi.  The highly anticipated debut follows Sooryavanshi's stellar IPL 2026 season.

description: A transatlantic debate ignites as Paris blames America's heavy air conditioning use for contributing to Europe's heatwave deaths. While the US mocks France's lack of AC, experts highlight America's significant emissions. The article contrasts this with the US's own struggles with extreme heat and a dismissive attitude towards climate science, underscoring the urgent need for sustainable cooling solutions.

title: Will Bronny James leave Los Angeles Lakers? Here’s what to know following LeBron James’ exit
description: LeBron James' decision to leave the Los Angeles Lakers has sparked fresh questions about Bronny James' future, but current signs point toward the young guard remaining with the franchise. Bronny's fully guaranteed contract, steady development over his first two NBA seasons and affordable salary make him a valuable depth option as the Lakers transition into a new era built around Luka Dončić and Austin Reaves. While speculation persists about a potential reunion with LeBron on his next team, Los Angeles is under no obligation to facilitate a move, leaving Bronny well positioned to continue carving out his own NBA career.

Russia turns to India for gasoline as fuel shortages deepen after Ukrainian strikes
description: The Kremlin said on Tuesday that it was in discussions with several countries about importing fuel at acceptable prices. One industry source said India has already shipped at least 60,000 metric tonnes of gasoline to Russia. Another source said two cargoes, each carrying between 30,000 and 40,000 tonnes, have been dispatched.

title: India-Bangladesh cricket ties back on track? BCB's media rights tender sparks hope
description: India and Bangladesh are poised to resume cricketing ties with a six-match white-ball series in September. The Bangladesh Cricket Board has initiated the process by floating a tender for media rights, signaling a potential thaw in relations after a year of strain. This development follows earlier postponements and diplomatic complexities, with the series tentatively scheduled to begin in early September.

description: Indian stock markets surged on Wednesday, with the Sensex and Nifty reclaiming key levels after a two-day slump. Positive global cues, including falling crude oil prices, boosted investor confidence. Sectors like banking and consumer goods saw significant gains, while IT stocks faced pressure. Analysts anticipate a positive second half of the year, citing easing geopolitical tensions and potential trade agreements.

Govt warns of strict action amid fraud concerns over WhatsApp usernames
description: Authorities have issued a stern warning to WhatsApp regarding its new username feature, emphasizing that the platform will be held legally accountable for any cyber fraud or impersonation facilitated by the update.  The government is scrutinizing the feature, which could allow users to adopt names similar to official entities, raising significant concerns about potential misuse.  WhatsApp, however, defends the update as a privacy enhancement, claiming built-in safeguards against abuse.

How Integrated Battle Groups could change the way Indian Army fights future wars
description: The Indian Army has launched a major structural overhaul, creating five Integrated Battle Groups and a Fire Support Group. This move aims to enhance rapid, decisive military responses by integrating infantry, armour, artillery, and logistics under single commanders. The reform, tested under the 17 Mountain Strike Corps, streamlines mobilisation and empowers field commanders for quicker action, building a more agile and lethal future-ready force.

title: Bengaluru’s NICE Road, Electronic City flyover toll fares revised: Here’s price list
Bata billed customer Rs 67 over MRP, told to refund and pay compensation
description: Bata India has been held liable by the Kerala consumer commission for overcharging a student Rs 67 above the printed MRP on a pair of shoes. The commission ruled that retailers cannot legally charge more than the MRP unless specific procedures, like clearly marking revised prices on the product and publishing in newspapers, are followed.

title: Army chief Dhiraj Seth unveils 'VIJAY' vision for future-ready Indian Army
description: General Seth, who took over as the 31st Chief of the Army Staff on Tuesday after General Upendra Dwivedi retired on June 30, addressed reporters after receiving a guard of honour at South Block. Speaking on his first day as Army chief, he described the Indian Army as a force that is fully prepared for combat. He said taking over the Army's top post was both an honour and a responsibility.

Despite a tough start in Ireland, Iyer is focused on instilling a fearless, aggressive brand of cricket. He aims to build a fresh team identity, learning from past experiences and the support staff to forge a relentless and determined unit ready for the challenges ahead.

description: Mahant Nritya Gopal Das, chairman of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, has been admitted to the ICU in Lucknow after being diagnosed with a urinary tract infection. The 89-year-old was found unconscious and is under critical care. This development comes just days before a crucial trust meeting on July 6, which will address the resignation of two trustees amid controversy over missing donations.

title: Bengaluru daycare horror: Toddlers put in washing machine, sprayed with toilet jet
description: Five women daycare staff at an IT company campus in Bengaluru face an FIR for allegedly abusing toddlers. Disturbing videos surfaced, showing caregivers threatening and physically mistreating children aged two to three. Allegations include locking children in bathrooms and using a washing machine. Police are questioning the accused and verifying the footage to determine the extent of the abuse.

Why Navies Sink Old Ships: US tests weapons on ex-USS Juneau; India runs similar drills
description: The US Navy recently sank the decommissioned USS Juneau in a "SinkEx" exercise off the Mariana Islands. This drill, involving air, surface, and submarine forces, tested weapon systems and provided crucial real-world training. Such exercises, also conducted by the Indian Navy, validate ordnance effectiveness and demonstrate operational readiness. Rear Admiral Eric Anduze highlighted the integration of joint capabilities for high-end Pacific operations.

title: Abhishek Sharma's year-long reign as World No. 1 ends in latest ICC T20I rankings
description: Ishan Kishan has claimed the top spot in the T20I batting rankings, edging out his teammate Abhishek Sharma with a remarkable performance. This is a notable achievement for Kishan, whose recent form, especially in the T20 World Cup, has been impressive. He now joins the ranks of elite Indian batsmen. Both players are preparing for the upcoming England series.

title: Centre issues show cause notice to Delhi Gymkhana Club in eviction proceedings
description: According to the notice, the Club's continued occupation of the land after the government resumed possession for a public purpose amounts to "unauthorized occupation" under Section 2(g) of the Public Premises (Eviction of Unauthorized Occupants) Act, 1971. The government has maintained that the President of India exercised powers under Clause 4 of the perpetual lease deed, which allows the lessor to resume the property if it is required for a public purpose.

description: Football witnessed a poignant echo of the past as Justin Kluivert, like his father Patrick 26 years prior, missed a crucial penalty, contributing to the Netherlands' shock FIFA World Cup 2026 exit against Morocco. The Dutch suffered their earliest ever World Cup elimination after a dramatic 3-2 penalty shootout loss. Morocco's heroics, including a late equaliser and a stellar goalkeeping performance, secured their place in the next round.

Watch: Australian goes to Thailand hotel with minor, comes out with body in suitcase
description: An Australian man, Simon Peter Carman, has been charged with the murder of a 17-year-old Thai girl in Pattaya. Her body was discovered in a suitcase near a railway line. CCTV footage reportedly shows Carman leaving a condominium with the suitcase before abandoning it. He was arrested attempting to flee the country and denies all charges, claiming the girl became 'crazy' and threatened him.

title: Hormuz oil shock sends India back to Russia: Is this a peak or the new normal?
description: Russia has emerged as the dominant supplier of crude oil for India - with imports touching over 240 million barrels in the four months since the US-Iran conflict began. In fact, crude imports from Russia are over 4 times the nearest supplier - UAE at over 58 million barrels.

description: Pets in urban India are no longer just companions; they've become integral family members, akin to children. This profound shift is evident in the burgeoning pet-care industry, offering everything from birthday parties to specialized healthcare.  This evolution reflects changing social dynamics, including nuclear families and increased urban loneliness, where pets provide vital emotional support and routine, fostering deep, reciprocal bonds.

title: 'Portrayed as Lord Krishna': BJP slams SP for 'mocking Hindus' over Akhilesh's birthday havan
description: The BJP has strongly criticized the Samajwadi Party, accusing them of disrespecting Hindus after workers depicted Akhilesh Yadav as Lord Krishna during a birthday ritual. This accusation comes amid controversy over alleged Ram Temple donation irregularities. Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath also attacked Yadav, urging him to visit the Ram Temple and recalling past SP actions against devotees.

No brain, heart, lungs: Indian sailor dies in Venezuela, kin says organs missing
description: A shocking discovery has emerged regarding the death of Indian seafarer Rakesh Chauhan in Venezuela. His body, returned to India without vital organs and with extensive prior stitching, has raised grave suspicions. The seafarers' union is demanding a thorough investigation into the alleged foul play and a cover-up, urging immediate intervention from the Indian Embassy for justice and compensation for the grieving family.

Days after Meta Layoffs, Zuckerberg says companies should empower workers, not replace them
description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, to offset significant investments in its AI "reboot." While Meta pours billions into AI infrastructure, Zuckerberg suggests a focus on human empowerment could create more jobs, a view contrasting with some industry leaders.

Hardik Pandya trade: CSK enter the race; seven franchises express interest
description: Seven IPL franchises have expressed interest in signing Hardik Pandya after the all-rounder decided to leave Mumbai Indians. Chennai Super Kings (CSK) have joined Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) and Rajasthan Royals in the race, with talks gathering pace. Player consent, leadership opportunities and trade structures are expected to shape one of the biggest IPL moves in recent years.

US govt lifts export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5; What Anthropic said
description: US government has lifted export controls on Anthropic's advanced AI models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, following national security concerns. The decision, made after weeks of tension and negotiations, allows trusted partners and federal agencies to access the models with new safeguards. Anthropic is restoring access globally, thanking users for their patience and highlighting collaborative efforts to refine AI safety.

Can landlords charge tenants for repainting walls? What Delhi High Court said
description: Tenants aren't always responsible for repainting or minor repairs upon moving out. A Delhi High Court ruling clarified that landlords must distinguish between normal wear and tear, which they cover, and actual damage caused by tenants. While routine painting is the landlord's duty, significant damage like removed fittings can be charged to the tenant, provided the landlord proves it and apportions costs fairly.

Siya, Chetan 'rehearsed' Ketan's murder on another hill before executing it at Lohagad Fort
description: In a shocking turn of events, Pune Rural police revealed that realtor Ketan Agarwal's fiancée, Siya Goyal, and her boyfriend, Chetan Chaudhary, allegedly rehearsed his murder before pushing him off a cliff at Lohagad Fort. The couple confessed to practicing the act on a hill near Market Yard. Police are planning a scene reconstruction and gait analysis of Chaudhary.

title: 13-year-old parrot battles rare egg-binding; family seeks treatment in Delhi
description: A 13-year-old parrot named Mithu faces a life-threatening condition as a large, calcified egg is stuck in its pelvic region, preventing natural laying.  Unable to find specialized avian surgery in Kolkata, Mithu's family is taking the bird to Delhi for an expensive operation.  Veterinarians are exploring affordable options to save the beloved pet, highlighting the challenges of reproductive disorders in caged birds.

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
description: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has divested his stake in a New York City real estate fund for a fraction of its value, selling for just six cents on the dollar. This significant loss comes as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze tightens conditions for landlords. The fund, A&E Real Estate, confirmed the sale, citing the challenging market for rent-stabilized apartments.
